user_prompt: hey how about not ripping apart the project for one little issue?
agent_reply: You’re right. I got too aggressive there. I backed out the “strip the firmware down” direction and kept the project intact.

The actual targeted fix is in [platformio.ini](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:16): I added the Arduino `Wire` include path so M5GFX sees `Wire.h` during compilation and uses the Arduino/legacy I2C path instead of pulling in the new IDF `i2c_master` driver.
Why this should fix the boot crash: before the change, the map showed both sides linked:
After the change, `i2c_new_master_bus` is gone from the map. Only the Arduino legacy I2C side remains, so IDF should no longer abort on the old/new driver conflict.
I kept the normal project libraries and flow in place. Build passes with `pio run -e m5stack-core2`.
